cards.back: A standard shift duration in many industries, typically representing the amount of time an employee works in a 24-hour period.
Common schedules include full-time, part-time, and rotating shifts, with varying hours of work and days off.
Physical and mental fatigue, reduced productivity, and decreased job satisfaction if not managed properly.
The distinction between a standard 8-hour shift and a full-time 40-hour workweek, with implications for employee compensation and benefits.
Labor laws and regulations, such as the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA), govern the minimum and maximum work hours, including overtime pay and rest periods.
